block_02: Here are 5 simple rules of risk management — follow them to gradually accumulate earnings and trade with relatively small risks.
block_03: Calculate the risk-return ratio per trade
block_04: The main components of money management are the “return” and “risk” parameters. They are interdependent: a high expected return is associated with a high risk, and vice versa. In most cases, the risk per trade varies in the range of 1-15% of the deposit.
block_11: For example, your deposit is $100. Then, in the case of a moderate risk approach, the allowable level of drawdown (price reduction) for one asset is $5. If the amount of the deposit has decreased to $80, the drawdown will also reduce to $4.
block_12: Set Stop Loss for every trade
block_13: This rule often puzzles amateur traders: sometimes, the position gets closed at the Stop Loss, and then the quotes go in the right direction. Use technical analysis to set SL at a safe level. If this level implies higher risk, reduce the entry volume.
block_16: Always ask yourself: will the asset be able to rise to the expected Take Profit level in order to fulfill the condition of the pledged risk-return ratio?
block_17: Try to limit your losses in time
block_18: For example, set a rule: no more than two losing trades on one asset during the week.
block_20: Losses must be calculated and controlled. If you have violated risk management rules and suffered losses, the best way out is to accept them — not to win them back and expose your deposit to additional risk.
